issues.title: Spring-ai does NOT work with io.github.bonigarcia:webdrivermanager:5.9.2
issues.body:
Whenever I have both dependencies         
```
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.ai</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-ai-openai-spring-boot-starter</artifactId>
            <version>0.8.1</version>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>io.github.bonigarcia</groupId>
            <artifactId>webdrivermanager</artifactId>
            <version>5.9.2</version>
        </dependency>
```
, I will have this issue:
```
19:45:22.310 [main] INFO org.springframework.test.context.support.AnnotationConfigContextLoaderUtils -- Could not detect default configuration classes for test class [guru.springframework.springaipromptengineering.ZeroAndFewShotTests]: ZeroAndFewShotTests does not declare any static, non-private, non-final, nested classes annotated with @Configuration.
```

I verified this issue in two of my projects. Here is one of the pom.xml:
```
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<project xmlns="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
         xsi:schemaLocation="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0 https://maven.apache.org/xsd/maven-4.0.0.xsd">
    <modelVersion>4.0.0</modelVersion>
    <parent>
        <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
        <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-parent</artifactId>
        <version>3.2.4</version>
        <relativePath/> <!-- lookup parent from repository -->
    </parent>
    <groupId>guru.springframework</groupId>
    <artifactId>spring-ai-prompt-engineering</artifactId>
    <version>0.0.1-SNAPSHOT</version>
    <properties>
        <java.version>21</java.version>
        <spring-ai.version>0.8.1</spring-ai.version>
    </properties>
    <dependencies>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-web</artifactId>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.ai</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-ai-openai-spring-boot-starter</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>io.github.bonigarcia</groupId>
            <artifactId>webdrivermanager</artifactId>
            <version>5.9.2</version>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-test</artifactId>
            <scope>test</scope>
            <exclusions>
                <exclusion>
                    <groupId>org.mockito</groupId>
                    <artifactId>mockito-core</artifactId>
                </exclusion>
                <exclusion>
                    <groupId>org.skyscreamer</groupId>
                    <artifactId>jsonassert</artifactId>
                </exclusion>
            </exclusions>
        </dependency>
    </dependencies>
    <dependencyManagement>
        <dependencies>
            <dependency>
                <groupId>org.springframework.ai</groupId>
                <artifactId>spring-ai-bom</artifactId>
                <version>${spring-ai.version}</version>
                <type>pom</type>
                <scope>import</scope>
            </dependency>
        </dependencies>
    </dependencyManagement>

    <build>
        <plugins>
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
                <artifactId>spring-boot-maven-plugin</artifactId>
            </plugin>
        </plugins>
    </build>
    <repositories>
        <repository>
            <id>spring-milestones</id>
            <name>Spring Milestones</name>
            <url>https://repo.spring.io/milestone</url>
            <snapshots>
                <enabled>false</enabled>
            </snapshots>
        </repository>
    </repositories>

</project>
```

comments.body:
I'm not sure what is going on here and need more information to debug, what code are you running

```
2024-08-01T19:45:29.244+02:00  WARN 5907 --- [spring-ai-prompt-engineering] [           main] o.s.a.a.r.SpringAiRetryAutoConfiguration : Retry error. Retry count:1

org.springframework.web.client.RestClientException: Error while extracting response for type [org.springframework.ai.openai.api.OpenAiApi$ChatCompletion] and content type [application/json]
	at org.springframework.web.client.DefaultRestClient.readWithMessageConverters(DefaultRestClient.java:236) ~[spring-web-6.1.5.jar:6.1.5]
	at org.springframework.web.client.DefaultRestClient$DefaultResponseSpec.readBody(DefaultRestClient.java:667) ~[spring-web-6.1.5.jar:6.1.5]
```

Can you provide a sample app that reproduces this?

```java
    @OperLog
    @CrossOrigin
    @GetMapping("/searchQualityEvent")
    public String searchQualityEvent(@RequestParam("message") String message) {
        UserMessage userMessage = new UserMessage(message);
        Prompt prompt = new Prompt(userMessage);
        String text = chatModel.call(prompt)
                .getResult()
                .getOutput()
                .getText();
        return chatClient.prompt()
                .messages(userMessage)
                .call()
                .content();
    }
```

```yml
spring:
  application:
    name: mcp-client
  ai:
    openai:
      chat:
        options:
          model: deepseek-chat
          parallel-tool-calls: true
      #      base-url: https://dashscope.aliyuncs.com/compatible-mode
      base-url: https://api.deepseek.com
```
I am using deepseek

@JasonQi-swe Spring AI 1.0 uses  jackson-databind `2.18.3` as well. Based on the response [here](https://github.com/bonigarcia/webdrivermanager/issues/1471#issuecomment-2904039660), this should be resolved. 

Closing this for now.

issues.title: Support OpenAI API parallel-tool-calls configuration
issues.body:
The OpenAI Function Calling API provides a property to control whether to enable [parallel function calling](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/function-calling/parallel-function-calling) during tool use.
Extend the OpenAiChatOptions to support this property.

issues.title: OpenAI Authentication API alignment
issues.body:
The OpenAI [Authentication](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/authentication)  API in addition to api-key supports the concepts for Organization ID and Project ID. 

>For users who belong to multiple organizations or are accessing their projects through their legacy user API key, you can pass a header to specify which organization and project is used for an API request. Usage from these API requests will count as usage for the specified organization and project.

Currently the Spring AI OpenAI Chat, Embedding, Image, Speech and Transcription models support only the api-key authentication key. 
- Add support for organization-id and project-id. 
- Align the implementation for common versus model-specific connection property merging for all Spring OpenAI Models.
- Alight the Spring Open AI properties documentation across all models. 
- Add new OpenAI ChatModel additional http-headers option to allow runtime HTTP headers overriding.
